name: From Via Nicolau de Sant Joan de l'Avellanet to Bagà – Circular Route
name_original: Vía Nicolau de Sant Joan de l'Avellanet a Bagà – Ruta Circular
region_intro: Hiking around Bagà offers access to the Parc Natural del Cadí-Moixeró, a region defined by the mountainous barrier of the Cadí and Moixeró ranges. The landscape features altitudes ranging from 900 meters in valleys to over 2,500 meters at its peaks, including the iconic Pedraforca Massif. Hikers can explore diverse terrain, from alpine meadows and mountain pine forests to deep valleys and sheer cliffs. Best hiking trails around Bagà The most popular hiking route is From Via Nicolau de Sant Joan de l'Avellanet to Bagà – Circular Route, a 5.0 miles (8.1 km) trail that takes 2 hours 31 minutes to complete. This circular route offers varied scenery around Bagà. Another top favourite among local hikers is Palau de Pinós – Sant Esteve Church loop from Bagà, a moderate 7.6 miles (12.2 km) path. This loop passes by historical sites and offers views of the surrounding landscape. Local hikers also love the Rebost Refuge – La Tossa Viewpoint loop from Gréixer, a 9.2 miles (14.8 km) trail leading through mountainous terrain, often completed in about 6 hours 54 minutes. Hiking around Bagà is defined by mountainous ranges, alpine meadows, and pine forests, with options for different ability levels. The routes in Bagà are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 170 reviews. More than 1600 hikers have used komoot to explore Bagà's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Bagà?
A: Bagà offers a wide variety of hiking experiences, with over 65 trails recorded on komoot. These routes cater to all levels, from easy strolls to challenging mountain treks within the Parc Natural del Cadí-Moixeró.

Q: Are there easy hiking options suitable for beginners or families in Bagà?
A: Yes, Bagà has 12 easy hiking routes, perfect for beginners or families looking for a relaxed outdoor experience. These trails typically involve less elevation gain and shorter distances, allowing everyone to enjoy the natural beauty of the area.

Q: What kind of natural features and landmarks can I expect to see on hikes around Bagà?
A: Hiking around Bagà provides access to diverse landscapes within the Parc Natural del Cadí-Moixeró. You can encounter dramatic mountain ranges like Cadí and Moixeró, alpine meadows, and dense pine forests. Notable natural features include the iconic Pedraforca Massif and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3069375" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Els Empedrats Waterfall and Gorge&lt;/a&gt;. You might also discover the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3557273" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Source of the Llobregat River&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any challenging hikes for experienced trekkers in the Bagà area?
A: Absolutely. For experienced hikers, Bagà offers 12 difficult routes that demand good fitness and sure-footedness. These trails often lead to higher altitudes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/104930" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Penyes Altes del Moixeró&lt;/a&gt;, and can involve significant elevation changes. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/1233320" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rebost Refuge – La Tossa Viewpoint loop from Gréixer&lt;/a&gt;, which is a challenging 14.8 km route.

Q: Can I find circular hiking routes around Bagà?
A: Yes, many of the trails around Bagà are circular, offering varied scenery without retracing your steps. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e939055571/from-via-nicolau-de-sant-joan-de-l-avellanet-to-baga-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;From Via Nicolau de Sant Joan de l'Avellanet to Bagà – Circular Route&lt;/a&gt;, which is an 8.1 km trail providing diverse views.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Bagà?
A: The best time to hike in Bagà largely depends on your preferred experience. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant landscapes, with alpine meadows blooming in spring. Summer is ideal for higher altitude treks, though it can be warmer. Winter hiking is possible, especially at lower elevations, but requires appropriate gear and awareness of snow conditions in the higher mountains.

Q: Are there any long-distance hiking routes that pass through or near Bagà?
A: Yes, Bagà is a significant point on some renowned long-distance routes. The historic &lt;em&gt;Camí dels Bons Homes&lt;/em&gt; (Path of the Good People/Cathar Route) passes through the region, tracing an ancient path across the Pyrenees. Additionally, the challenging hut-to-hut &lt;em&gt;Cavalls del Vent&lt;/em&gt; circular route, which includes opportunities to climb Pedraforca, is also in the vicinity.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Bagà?
A: The hiking trails in Bagà are highly regarded by the komoot community, holding an impressive average rating of 4.8 stars from over 170 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse terrain, stunning mountain views, and the well-maintained network of paths that cater to various skill levels.

Q: Are there any cultural or historical sites to explore while hiking near Bagà?
A: Beyond its natural beauty, the Bagà region is rich in cultural heritage. Bagà itself features medieval walls and a historic porticoed square. You can also visit the Benedictine monastery of Sant Llorenç prop Bagà. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10470693" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Palau de Pinós – Sant Esteve Church loop from Bagà&lt;/a&gt; is an example of a route that passes by historical sites.

Q: Are dogs allowed on the hiking trails around Bagà?
A: Generally, dogs are welcome on many hiking trails in the Parc Natural del Cadí-Moixeró, but it's always advisable to keep them on a leash, especially in areas with livestock or protected wildlife. Always check local regulations for specific trails or protected zones within the park to ensure a pleasant and responsible hike with your dog.

Q: Are there any hikes that offer panoramic viewpoints?
A: Many trails around Bagà lead to spectacular viewpoints, offering panoramic vistas of the Cadí and Moixeró ranges, and the iconic Pedraforca Massif. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39249555" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Niu de l'Àliga Refuge – La Tossa Viewpoint loop from la Molina&lt;/a&gt; is one such route designed to showcase breathtaking views.

Q: Is there parking available for hikers in Bagà?
A: Yes, Bagà, as a gateway to the Parc Natural del Cadí-Moixeró, typically offers parking facilities for hikers. Specific trailheads or village centers often have designated parking areas. It's recommended to arrive early during peak seasons to secure a spot.

Q: Can I combine a hike with a visit to a mountain refuge?
A: Absolutely. The region features several mountain refuges that can serve as destinations or stopovers during your hike. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/949301" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lluís Estasen Refuge&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/232972" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Niu de l'Àliga Refuge&lt;/a&gt; are popular options, providing a chance to rest and enjoy the mountain environment. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6956075" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Font de Paller – Santuari de Paller loop from Bagà&lt;/a&gt; also offers a scenic journey.
tips: Small picnic area (with tables, barbecues and shade) very close to the hermitage of San Juan del Avellanet that gives it its name. Access is on the road that goes from Bagà to Gisclareny.

The Via del Nicolau was built at the beginning of the 20th century (1914-1916) in order to transport wood from Gisclareny to Guardiola de Berguedà. It was disused for years and in 2009 the path was restored and today we can enjoy a walk through several tunnels.

The Vía del Nicolau, was built at the beginning of the 20th century (1914-1916) in order to transport wood from Gisclareny to Guardiola de Berguedà, it was disused for years and in 2009 the path was restored and today we can enjoy a walk passing several tunnels.
The Pasarella del Nicolau is a 35-meter suspension bridge. Very funny!

waypoints.name: Sant Joan del Avellanet Recreation Area
